framer
z:warnings.showwarning() must be set to a function or methodN)
	NameError
_showwarning_orig
callable
	TypeErrorr
_showwarnmsg`
_formatwarning_origr
Insert an entry into the list of warnings filters (at the front).
    'action' -- one of "error", "ignore", "always", "default", "module",
                or "once"
    'message' -- a regex that the warning message must match
    'category' -- a class that the warning must be a subclass of
    'module' -- a regex that the module name must match
    'lineno' -- an integer line number, 0 matches all warnings
    'append' -- if true, append to the list of filters
    r
append)
compile
_add_filter)
actionr
moduler
Insert a simple entry into the list of warnings filters (at the front).
    A simple filter matches all modules and messages.
    'action' -- one of "error", "ignore", "always", "default", "module",
                or "once"
    'category' -- a class that the warning must be a subclass of
    'lineno' -- an integer line number, 0 matches all warnings
    'append' -- if true, append to the list of filters
    Nr7
filters
remove
ValueError
insertr8
_filters_mutated)
itemr
zAClear the list of warning filters, so that no filters are active.N)

altkeyr
_category_name)
selfr
__init__
WarningMessage.__init__c
NzD{message : %r, category : %r, filename : %r, lineno : %s, line : %r})
__str__
WarningMessage.__str__)
NNN)
_WARNING_DETAILSr
A context manager that copies and restores the warnings filter upon
    exiting the context.
    The 'record' argument specifies whether warnings should be captured by a
    custom implementation of warnings.showwarning() and be appended to a list
    returned by the context manager. Otherwise None is returned by the context
    manager. The objects appended to the list are arguments whose attributes
    mirror the arguments to showwarning().
    The 'module' argument is to specify an alternative module to the module
    named 'warnings' and imported under that name. This argument is only useful
    when testing the warnings module itself.
    FN)
recordr>
Specify whether to record warnings and if an alternative module
        should be used other than sys.modules['warnings'].
        For compatibility with Python 3.0, please consider all arguments to be
        keyword-only.
        N
warningsF)
_recordr
modules
_module
_entered)
catch_warnings.__init__c
record=Truer
z	module=%rz
%s(%s)z
